name: Karateclub (Unsupervised Graph ML)

performance.notes: Depends on graph size and algorithm; uses NumPy/SciPy under the hood

agent_readiness.notes: sklearn-like unsupervised graph ML (node embeddings, graph embeddings, community detection). from karateclub import Node2Vec, DeepWalk, HOPE, BoostNE, GEMSEC. model = Node2Vec(). model.fit(graph). embeddings = model.get_embedding(). GraphWave for diffusion embeddings. Community detection: NNSED, DANMF, M-NMF. Graph-level embeddings: Graph2Vec, WL kernel. NetworkX graph required. 30+ algorithms from graph ML literature. sklearn-compatible API (fit/transform). Best for research and prototyping graph ML.

gotchas: GPL-3.0 license restricts commercial embedding in proprietary software
Requires NetworkX graph — conversion from other formats needed
Some algorithms have high memory requirements for large graphs
best_when: Research experimentation with 30+ graph ML algorithms
Node/graph embeddings for downstream ML tasks
Rapid prototyping without implementing algorithms from scratch
Learning graph ML — well-documented sklearn-like API
avoid_when: Large-scale production (PyTorch Geometric or DGL better)
When specific deep GNN models needed (GCN, GAT — use PyG)
summary: Karateclub is an sklearn-like library for unsupervised graph ML with 30+ algorithms. model = Node2Vec(); model.fit(nx_graph); model.get_embedding(). DeepWalk, HOPE, BoostNE for node embeddings. Graph2Vec for graph-level embeddings. Community detection: NNSED, DANMF. NetworkX graph required. GPL-3.0. Best for research prototyping of graph embeddings without implementing from scratch. Use PyTorch Geometric for production deep GNNs.
